title: Internal unstacker for a heat treatment furnace
abstract: A system for applying a thermal treatment to metal components, such as castings or forgings, loaded within a stack of containers includes a heat treatment furnace having heating/soak zone(s), an unstacking zone, and an exit zone with an exit door at an end thereof. The furnace includes a first conveyor configured to carry the stack at a first speed through the heating/soak zone(s) and a second conveyor extending between the first conveyor and the exit zone that is configured to receive the stack at the first speed from the first conveyor. The furnace also includes a lifting apparatus within the unstacking zone that lifts one or more upper loaded containers in the stack off of one or more lower loaded containers, after which the second conveyor carries the lower loaded containers away from the unstacking zone toward the exit door at a second speed that is greater than the first speed.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for processing metal components loaded within a plurality of containers, the containers being stacked one on top of another to form a stack of loaded containers, the system comprising: an unstacking zone; a first conveyor configured to carry a stack of loaded containers; a second conveyor configured to receive a stack of loaded containers from the first conveyor; and a lifting apparatus within the unstacking zone and configured to lift an at least one upper container of a stack of loaded containers off of an at least one lower container of a stack of loaded containers. 2. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a heat treatment furnace including: at least one heating/soak zone; the unstacking zone; and an exit zone having an exit door for the heat treatment furnace at an end thereof; wherein the first conveyor is configured to carry a stack of loaded containers at a first speed through the at least one heating/soak zone; the second conveyor extends between the first conveyor and the exit zone and is configured to receive a stack of loaded containers at the first speed from the first conveyor; and wherein the second conveyor is configured to carry an at least one lower container of a stack of loaded containers away from the unstacking zone toward the exit door at a second speed greater than the first speed. 3. The system of claim 2, further comprising a quench station located downstream of the exit zone and configured to receive an at least one loaded container separated from a stack of loaded containers and to quench the components loaded therein. 4. A system for applying a thermal treatment to metal components loaded within a plurality of containers stacked one on top of another to form a stack of loaded containers, the system comprising: 

 a heat treatment furnace including: at least one heating/soak zone; an unstacking zone; and an exit zone having an exit door for the heat treatment furnace at an end thereof; a first conveyor configured to carry the stack of loaded containers at a first speed through the at least one heating/soak zone; a second conveyor extending between the first conveyor and the exit zone and configured to receive the stack at the first speed from the first conveyor; and a lifting apparatus within the unstacking zone and configured to lift at least one upper loaded container in the stack off of at least one lower loaded container, wherein the second conveyor is configured to carry the at least one lower loaded container away from the unstacking zone toward the exit door at a second speed greater than the first speed. 5. A method for processing metal components loaded within a plurality of containers stacked one on top of another to form a stack of loaded containers, the method comprising: moving the stack on a first conveyor, transferring the stack from the first conveyor to a second conveyor; within the unstacking zone, lifting an at least one upper container in the stack off of an at least one lower container; and moving the at least one lower container. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising: loading the stack of loaded containers into a heat treatment furnace including at least one heating/soak zone, the unstacking zone, and an exit zone having an exit door to the heat treatment furnace at an end thereof; moving the stack downstream through the at least one soak zone and toward the unstacking zone on the first conveyor operating at a first speed; transferring the stack from the first conveyor to the second conveyor upon the stack reaching the unstacking zone; and moving the at least one lower loaded container away from the unstacking zone toward the exit door on the second conveyor operating at a second speed that is greater than the first speed. 7. The method of claim 5, further comprising: moving the at least one lower loaded container from the exit door into a quench station located downstream of the exit zone; and quenching the components loaded within the at least one lower loaded container. 8. A method for processing metal components loaded within a plurality of containers stacked one on top of another to form a stack of loaded containers within a thermal treatment system, the method comprising: loading the stack of loaded containers into a heat treatment furnace including at least one heating/soak zone, an unstacking zone, and an exit zone having an exit door to the heat treatment furnace at an end thereof; moving the stack downstream through the at least one soak zone and toward the unstacking zone on a first conveyor operating at a first speed; upon the stack reaching the unstacking zone, transferring the stack from the first conveyor to a second conveyor; within the unstacking zone, lifting at least one upper loaded container in the stack off of at least one lower loaded container; and moving the at least one lower loaded container away from the unstacking zone and toward the exit door on the second conveyor operating at a second speed that is greater than the first speed.
